# snappyHexMesh: surface to verified mesh (Foundation v10)

The pathway from an imported STL to a quality-verified mesh. Every command,
flag and dictionary shape below was verified live on Foundation v10 (the
meshing harvest) or taken from the shipped v10 tutorials — follow it, not
folklore. Two vocabulary pins up front: the v10 feature-edge utility is
**`surfaceFeatures`** reading `system/surfaceFeaturesDict`
(`surfaceFeatureExtract` is the ESI-side name — an installation that only
has that one is ESI; see
[openfoam-v10-conventions.md](openfoam-v10-conventions.md)), and the v10
template vocabulary for the keep-point is **`insidePoint`**, not the
classic `locationInMesh` seen in older material.

## The sequence

Run each meshing stage as its own `run_openfoam_command` call — separate
calls keep the failing stage identifiable and its log separate:

1. **Inspect the surface first**: `inspect_stl` on the source STL —
   watertight verdict, shell count, bounding box, units suspicion. A
   non-watertight surface is a geometry problem: fix the geometry, do not
   tune snappy around it.
2. **Import**: `import_geometry` places the surface under the case's
   `constant/triSurface/` — with a deterministic scale when the units are
   wrong (below).
3. **Write the dicts** with `write_case_file` (worked blocks below):
   `system/blockMeshDict`, `system/surfaceFeaturesDict`,
   `system/snappyHexMeshDict`, `system/meshQualityDict`.
4. `blockMesh` — the background hex mesh.
5. `surfaceFeatures` — reads `system/surfaceFeaturesDict`, writes
   `constant/triSurface/<name>.eMesh`.
6. `snappyHexMesh -overwrite` — castellate, snap, optionally add layers.
   `-overwrite` writes the mesh in place (`constant/polyMesh`) instead of
   into numbered time directories.
7. **Verify**: `assess_mesh` (read it with the concave-cells rule below),
   and `read_mesh_boundaries` — the surface must appear as its own patch
   with the `patchInfo` type. The harvested cube case: 3393 cells, the
   cube a proper `wall` patch with 384 faces.

A healthy run: every stage exits 0 and the snappy log ends
`Finished meshing without any errors`. Exit 0 is NOT proof you meshed the
intended region — see the insidePoint pitfalls.

Units: STL carries no unit metadata — suspicion comes only from
bounding-box extents (a 1 m part arriving as extent 1000 is mm-authored
CAD). The fix is deterministic, never a hand-edited dict: import with
scale 0.001, which runs the v10 form

```
surfaceTransformPoints "scale=(0.001 0.001 0.001)" cube_mm.stl cube_scaled.stl
```

— a transformations STRING. The `-scale '(...)'` flag form from older docs
does not exist on v10. Verified live: bbox (1000 1000 1000) → (1 1 1),
surface stays closed, exit 0.

## Background mesh: sizing rules, then the dict

The background mesh is a plain box of roughly CUBIC cells
(`simpleGrading (1 1 1)`) — refinement halves cells per level, so any
background anisotropy propagates to the surface and degrades snapping.

- **Padding** (external flow, body of characteristic length L): ~5L
  upstream and lateral, 10–15L downstream for the wake. Internal flow: the
  box just needs to fully enclose the surface with margin.
- **Base cell size**: start near L/10 — the body should span several
  background cells in each direction, so castellation has something to
  refine. The cell size at the surface is base/2^level; pick the surface
  refinement level so the smallest geometric feature you care about spans
  at least a couple of refined cells.
- Every added refinement level costs ~8x the cells in the refined volume —
  raise levels deliberately, not defensively.

Worked block (1 m body near the origin; all dict blocks in this reference
are the file's full meaningful content — give each the usual `FoamFile`
header with `format ascii; class dictionary; object <name>;`):

```
xMin  -5;   xMax  15;
yMin  -5;   yMax   5;
zMin  -5;   zMax   5;
xCells 40;  yCells 20;  zCells 20;    // 0.5 m cells, cubic

convertToMeters 1;

vertices
(
    ($xMin $yMin $zMin) ($xMax $yMin $zMin) ($xMax $yMax $zMin) ($xMin $yMax $zMin)
    ($xMin $yMin $zMax) ($xMax $yMin $zMax) ($xMax $yMax $zMax) ($xMin $yMax $zMax)
);

blocks
(
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) ($xCells $yCells $zCells) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

boundary
(
    inlet      { type patch; faces ((0 3 7 4)); }
    outlet     { type patch; faces ((1 5 6 2)); }
    freestream { type patch; faces ((4 7 6 5) (3 2 6 7) (0 4 5 1) (0 1 2 3)); }
);
```

## Feature edges: surfaceFeaturesDict

```
surfaces ("cube.stl");

includedAngle 150;
```

`surfaceFeatures` writes `constant/triSurface/cube.eMesh`. 150 degrees is
the shipped-tutorial default: edges whose faces meet more sharply than
that are captured as features; tessellation noise on smooth surfaces is
not. The eMesh does nothing until the snappy dict both lists it AND
switches snapping to explicit — the .cfg default is implicit (next
section); forgetting the switches silently ignores your eMesh.

## The snappy dict: minimal, on top of the shipped .cfg

Two files. `system/meshQualityDict` is one line (the .cfg below pulls
`${FOAM_CASE}/system/meshQualityDict`, so the file must exist):

```
#includeEtc "caseDicts/mesh/generation/meshQualityDict"
```

`system/snappyHexMeshDict` opens with the shipped defaults file, which
supplies every control default — the user dict then carries only: the
three stage switches, `geometry`, `refinementSurfaces` (+ `patchInfo`),
`refinementRegions` (empty is fine), `features` + `insidePoint` in
`castellatedMeshControls`, and the snap-mode overrides:

```
#includeEtc "caseDicts/mesh/generation/snappyHexMeshDict.cfg"

castellatedMesh on;
snap            on;
addLayers       off;

geometry
{
    cube
    {
        type triSurfaceMesh;
        file "cube.stl";
    }
};

castellatedMeshControls
{
    features
    (
        { file "cube.eMesh"; level 2; }
    );

    refinementSurfaces
    {
        cube
        {
            level (2 2);
            patchInfo { type wall; }
        }
    }

    refinementRegions {}

    insidePoint (10.13 0.21 0.17);   // in the FLUID, off round coordinates
}

snapControls
{
    explicitFeatureSnap true;
    implicitFeatureSnap false;
}
```

- The `geometry` entry name (`cube`) is what `refinementSurfaces` keys on,
  and `patchInfo { type wall; }` is what makes the surface arrive as a
  real `wall` patch in the final mesh.
- **Feature snap**: the .cfg defaults are `implicitFeatureSnap true;
  explicitFeatureSnap false;`. For eMesh-based (explicit) feature
  snapping, override BOTH — as in the block — and list the eMesh under
  `features`. Explicit snapping with the extracted edges is what keeps
  sharp edges sharp; the fallback to the implicit default is a downgrade
  rung, not a starting point, for geometry with real edges.
- `addLayers off` is the conservative start: get castellate + snap to a
  verified mesh first, then add layers as a separate, separately-judged
  step (below).

## Refinement guidance

- `level (min max)` on a refinement surface: min applies everywhere on the
  surface; max applies where the surface turns faster than
  `resolveFeatureAngle` (a .cfg-supplied default). `(2 2)` is the
  conservative start; raise toward `(3 3)` when the castellated
  approximation of curved regions is visibly blocky.
- Feature-edge `level` in `features`: match the surface min level as a
  default.
- `nCellsBetweenLevels` (castellatedMeshControls): buffer cells between
  successive refinement levels — the .cfg supplies a default; raise it
  (shipped external-aero tutorials use up to 5) when `assess_mesh` shows
  non-orthogonality or skewness concentrated at refinement transitions.
- Volume refinement uses `refinementRegions` keyed on extra `geometry`
  entries (e.g. a wake box `wake { type searchableBox; min (...); max
  (...); }`), with `mode inside; level 2;` or distance-based
  `mode distance; levels ((0.2 5));`.

**Layers** (only after the snapped mesh verifies): `addLayers on;` plus

```
addLayersControls
{
    layers
    {
        cube
        {
            nSurfaceLayers 2;
        }
    }

    relativeSizes       on;
    expansionRatio      1.2;
    finalLayerThickness 0.5;
    minThickness        1e-3;
}
```

Layer knobs trade coverage against cell quality: judge the result with
`assess_mesh` — `max_aspect_ratio` is the metric layers move first, and
`expansionRatio` / `finalLayerThickness` / `minThickness` are its knobs
(the snappyHexMesh column in [mesh-quality.md](mesh-quality.md)). Layers
that would violate quality controls are locally collapsed rather than
failing the run — read the snappy log before trusting that layers exist
where you asked.

## insidePoint pitfalls

`insidePoint` tells castellation which side of the surface to KEEP.

- **Wrong side**: a point inside the body (or inside a cavity) meshes that
  region instead of the fluid — and snappy still exits 0 with
  `Finished meshing without any errors`. Detect it from the verdict facts:
  an absurd cell count in the `assess_mesh` census, or
  `read_mesh_boundaries` missing the expected patch.
- **On a face or edge**: the point must fall strictly inside a background
  cell; a point exactly on a cell face/edge of the blockMesh grid is
  ambiguous and snappy errors on it. Background faces sit on round
  coordinates (the block above: multiples of 0.5) — pick off-round
  coordinates, as in the worked dict.
- **Too close to the surface**: a point within a cell size of the surface
  can land in a cell that castellation removes; keep it clearly in the
  fluid.
- **After rescaling**: a point chosen for the mm-scaled geometry is far
  outside the domain once the surface is scaled to metres — recheck
  `insidePoint` whenever the import scale changes.

## Verifying: assess_mesh and the concave-cells rule

`assess_mesh` runs `checkMesh -allTopology -allGeometry`
([mesh-quality.md](mesh-quality.md) is the judgement layer). One
snappy-specific rule on top, verified live: **a HEALTHY snapped mesh fails the
concave-cells check.** The harvest's clean cube mesh reported
`***Concave cells (using face planes) found, number of cells: 192` →
`Failed 1 mesh checks.` — parsed as a `failed` verdict with the metric
`concave_cells_found`. Concave cells are expected on castellated/snapped
meshes; snappy's own quality dict allows `maxConcave 80`.

The rule: a `failed` verdict whose ONLY failing metric is
`concave_cells_found` (checkMesh's `failed_checks` = 1) reads as a PASS on
a snappy mesh. Every other failing metric — and everything about a
`warnings` verdict — keeps its full [mesh-quality.md](mesh-quality.md)
meaning.

## The downgrade ladder (failed metric → knob turn)

Work the failing `assess_mesh` metric, not the whole recipe. The full knob
detail lives in the snappyHexMesh column of
[mesh-quality.md](mesh-quality.md)'s knob table — this ladder maps each
metric to its FIRST turn there and the escalation:

| Failing metric | First turn | If it persists |
|---|---|---|
| `max_non_orthogonality` | more castellated refinement at the surface (raise `refinementSurfaces` level) | gentler snapControls (`nSmoothPatch`, `nRelaxIter`, `tolerance`) |
| `max_skewness` | snapControls smoothing | raise the castellation level at the offending surface |
| `max_aspect_ratio` | addLayersControls: `expansionRatio`, `finalLayerThickness`, `minThickness` | drop layers (`addLayers off`) and re-judge |
| `min_face_area` / `min_volume` | clean the geometry — self-intersections, gaps (`inspect_stl` again) | meshQualityControls, as a last resort |
| `min_edge_length` | feature-edge cleanup; `resolveFeatureAngle` | raise `includedAngle` so fewer noise edges are extracted |
| `number_of_regions` > 1 | castellation leaked through a geometry gap — close the surface | — |
| `concave_cells_found` alone | not a failure on a snappy mesh (rule above) | — |
| any topology failure | regenerate — the mesh is malformed; sizing knobs will not help | — |

When knob turns stop converging, downgrade ambition one rung at a time and
re-verify at each rung: (1) drop layers; (2) drop explicit feature snap
back to the .cfg's implicit default (accepting softer edges); (3) run
castellated-only (`snap off`) to isolate whether the background/refinement
sizing is the problem. Climb back up once the lower rung verifies.

## Parallel meshing (brain-only, via run_openfoam_command)

For meshes big enough that snappy approaches `run_openfoam_command`'s
default 600 s timeout, mesh in parallel — no new tools, `run_case`
untouched. Verified live end-to-end; the parallel mesh was IDENTICAL to
the serial one (3393 cells on the harvest fixture).

`system/decomposeParDict` — decomposePar works on a mesh-only case, no
`0/` directory needed (the verified 2-way form; `n` must multiply to
`numberOfSubdomains`):

```
numberOfSubdomains 2;

method hierarchical;

hierarchicalCoeffs
{
    n     (2 1 1);
    order xyz;
}
```

Then three `run_openfoam_command` calls, the snappy one with a raised
per-call `timeout` (e.g. 3600):

1. `decomposePar`
2. `mpirun --allow-run-as-root -np 2 snappyHexMesh -overwrite -parallel`
3. `reconstructParMesh -constant`

- **`--allow-run-as-root` is required**: the Foam-Agent container runs as
  root and plain `mpirun` refuses (exit 1, "mpirun has detected an attempt
  to run as root."). `-np` must equal `numberOfSubdomains`.
- `reconstructParMesh -constant` matches `-overwrite`: the mesh lives in
  `constant/polyMesh`, so reconstruct the constant directory. Run
  `assess_mesh` after reconstruction, and clear stale `processor*`
  directories before any later decomposition of the case.
- Stdin pitfall: `mpirun` consumes its script's remaining stdin when a
  multi-command script is piped into a shell — one more reason each stage
  is its own `run_openfoam_command` call (single commands are unaffected);
  in script files, feed mpirun `< /dev/null`.
- Scope: this recipe is for MESHING only. Parallel solver runs block a
  tool call for the whole solve and are deferred to the background-polling
  capability — do not launch a solver under mpirun through
  `run_openfoam_command`.

## Common snappy failure modes (for the debugger)

| Symptom | Likely cause / fix |
|---|---|
| snappy FATAL: cannot find `.../constant/triSurface/<name>.eMesh` | `surfaceFeatures` not run, or the `features` file entry doesn't match the surface name — the order is blockMesh → surfaceFeatures → snappy |
| Sharp edges come out rounded despite an eMesh | the .cfg defaults (`implicitFeatureSnap true; explicitFeatureSnap false;`) still active — override BOTH switches and list the eMesh under `features` |
| `assess_mesh` `failed`, only failing metric `concave_cells_found` | normal on a snapped mesh — treat as pass (rule above) |
| Absurd cell count; expected patch missing from `read_mesh_boundaries` | `insidePoint` on the wrong side of the surface — snappy meshed the other region and still exited 0 |
| snappy errors mentioning the inside point | point coincides with a background cell face/edge — move it off round coordinates |
| Surface bbox ~1000x the domain; castellation refines nothing sensible | mm-authored STL (no unit metadata in STL) — reimport with scale 0.001 (`surfaceTransformPoints` transformations string above) |
| Mesh floods both sides / `number_of_regions` > 1 | non-watertight surface: castellation leaks through the gap — `inspect_stl` catches it before meshing; fix the geometry, not snappy |
| Cryptic dictionary parse error on a dict authored on Windows | CRLF line endings — strip CRs (`sed -i 's/\r$//' system/<dict>` via `run_openfoam_command`) |
| `mpirun` exits 1: "attempt to run as root" | add `--allow-run-as-root` (required in the Foam-Agent container) |
| `run_openfoam_command` times out mid-snappy | raise the call's `timeout` parameter; for real size, go parallel (recipe above) |

Downstream of a verified mesh: the patch names created by `patchInfo` and
the blockMeshDict boundary are what every `0/` field file must match —
check with `read_mesh_boundaries` and follow the cross-file consistency
rules in [file-generation.md](file-generation.md). The fix-loop discipline
(history of error → diagnosis → fix, never repeating a failed fix) is
[error-playbook.md](error-playbook.md).
